message:
fix(rch/hook): shell-safe argv reassembly for `rch exec -- ...` via shell_words::join

`run_exec(command_parts: Vec<String>)` reassembles the parsed argv back
into a single command string that is subsequently handed to the hook
path (which invokes `sh -c <command>` under the hood). It used
`command_parts.join(" ")`.

Plain space-joining is wrong whenever an argv entry contains any byte
that the shell treats as special:

  - spaces (the outermost shell already stripped the original quoting,
    so `--features='foo bar'` arrives as a single argv with a literal
    space; `sh -c` then re-splits it into two tokens and
    `--features=foo` gets a separate argument `bar`).
  - quotes, `$`, backticks (variable / command substitution triggered
    on re-parse).
  - `;`, `&`, `|`, `>`, `<` (shell metacharacters that change control
    flow).
  - backslash (escape-handled on re-parse, silently mangling filenames).

In practice this bit `rch exec -- cargo build --features='foo bar'`
and any invocation whose argv contained `$PATH`, semicolons in build
scripts, or quoted file paths. The failure mode is subtle: the outer
command succeeds but with corrupted inner argv, so the build runs with
subtly wrong flags.

Fix: new helper `join_exec_command(&[String]) -> String` that delegates
to `shell_words::join`, which quotes each argv entry with POSIX-safe
rules so a subsequent `shell_words::split` (which is what `sh -c`'s
word splitting is functionally equivalent to) reproduces the original
argv byte-for-byte. `shell_words` is already a transitive dep via
other paths in this crate, no new dependency.

Regression tests:
  - `join_exec_command_plain_args_unchanged` — round-trip identity
    for argv with no metacharacters.
  - `join_exec_command_preserves_space_bearing_arg` — the
    `--features=foo bar` case, the original bug report shape.
  - `join_exec_command_preserves_quote_metachars` — round-trip for
    argv containing `"`, `$PATH`, `;`, confirming every byte survives.
  - `join_exec_command_empty_input` — empty argv yields empty string
    (matches prior `.join(" ")` behaviour so the `command.is_empty()`
    guard below continues to fire correctly).

No call-site changes outside `run_exec` itself.
